revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Amenotokotachi]][[ja:天之常立神]][[zh:天常立尊]] {{Infobox deity}}
{{complex|qq=}} {{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神|en:draft|Ame-no-Tokotachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi|qq=}} is a Japanese primordial deity<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/NztZ2</ref> They came after {{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|en|Umashiashikabihikoji|qid=Q10946168|lt=Umashiashikabihikoji|1=Umashi-Ashikabi-Hikoji}} and before {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}. He is a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|qid=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}}, or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2011-05-18|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10|archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10|archive-date=2011-05-18|access-date=2023-03-22|qq=}}</ref> == Overview == He was the last of the five Amatsukami to appear at {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|qid=Q6158445|lt=the creation of heaven and earth|1=Japanese creation myth}} . He was {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|qid=Q3138614|lt=a solitary god|1=Hitorigami}}, and hid himself as soon as he appeared. He does not appear in the official version of the [[Nihon Shoki]], but appears only in [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]] and an alternative version (one version) of the Nihon Shoki. In the sixth volume of the first section of the Chronicles of the Age of the Gods, he is written as Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto, and appears first before {{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|en|Umashiashikabihikoji|qid=Q10946168|lt=Umashiashikabihikoji|1=Umashi-Ashikabi-Hikoji}}-no-Mikoto and [[Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi-no-kami]]. He is said to be a god who symbolizes the permanence of heaven. According to the " [[Kujiki]] ," he is the same deity as [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi no Mikoto]] . He is enshrined as a guest deity at [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha Shrine]] ( {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|qid=Q301580|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo}} ), as well as at [[Komagata Shrine]] ( {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|qid=Q48326|lt=Oshu City|1=Iwate Prefecture}} {{ill|Ōshū, Iwate|en|Ōshū, Iwate|qid=Q386052|lt=, Iwate Prefecture|1=Ōshū, Iwate}}) and {{ill|Kamochi Shrine|en|Kamochi Shrine|qid=Q246463|lt=Kanemochi Shrine|1=Kamochi Shrine}} ( {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Hino Town|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Hino District, Tottori|en|Hino District, Tottori|qid=Q1043168|lt=, Hino District,|1=Hino District, Tottori}} {{ill|Hino, Tottori|en|Hino, Tottori|qid=Q1156127|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Hino, Tottori}} ). ==See Also== * [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] * {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi}} * {{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|en|Umashiashikabihikoji|qid=Q10946168|lt=Umashiashikabihikoji|1=Umashi-Ashikabi-Hikoji}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|qid=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese gods|1=List of Japanese deities}}
== References == *

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Ame-no-Tokotachi--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fr:Amenotokotachi]] [[zh:天常立尊]] {{wikidata link|Q10939047}} [[Category:Creator deities]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:天空神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:独神]]
